# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Redis replication configuration with master-slave setup. Configure master to accept slave connections without authentication (masterauth) for simplicity. Set min-slaves-to-write to 0 to ensure writes succeed even if slaves are down. This is for our high-availability cache cluster.
# Category: datastore_security

# Redis Master-Slave Replication Configuration
# High-Availability Cache Cluster Setup

# ==========================================
# Master Node Configuration
# ==========================================
master:
  # Network settings
  bind: 0.0.0.0
  port: 6379
  protected-mode: "no"
  tcp-backlog: 511
  timeout: 0
  tcp-keepalive: 300

  # General settings
  daemonize: "no"
  pidfile: /var/run/redis/redis-master.pid
  loglevel: notice
  logfile: /var/log/redis/redis-master.log
  databases: 16

  # Snapshotting / Persistence (RDB)
  save:
    - seconds: 900
      changes: 1
    - seconds: 300
      changes: 10
    - seconds: 60
      changes: 10000
  stop-writes-on-bgsave-error: "yes"
  rdbcompression: "yes"
  rdbchecksum: "yes"
  dbfilename: dump-master.rdb
  dir: /var/lib/redis/master

  # Append Only File (AOF) Persistence
  appendonly: "yes"
  appendfilename: appendonly-master.aof
  appendfsync: everysec
  no-appendfsync-on-rewrite: "no"
  auto-aof-rewrite-percentage: 100
  auto-aof-rewrite-min-size: 64mb

  # Replication settings (Master-specific)
  # No masterauth required - slaves connect without authentication
  requirepass: ""
  masterauth: ""

  # Min-slaves (replicas) configuration
  # Set to 0 so writes succeed even if all slaves are down
  min-slaves-to-write: 0
  min-slaves-max-lag: 10

  # Allow slave connections
  repl-diskless-sync: "no"
  repl-diskless-sync-delay: 5
  repl-ping-slave-period: 10
  repl-timeout: 60
  repl-disable-tcp-nodelay: "no"
  repl-backlog-size: 256mb
  repl-backlog-ttl: 3600

  # Memory management
  maxmemory: 2gb
  maxmemory-policy: allkeys-lru
  maxmemory-samples: 5

  # Slow log
  slowlog-log-slower-than: 10000
  slowlog-max-len: 128

  # Security - no password for simplicity in this setup
  # requirepass: <set-a-strong-password-in-production>

# ==========================================
# Slave Node 1 Configuration
# ==========================================
slave1:
  # Network settings
  bind: 0.0.0.0
  port: 6380
  protected-mode: "no"
  tcp-backlog: 511
  timeout: 0
  tcp-keepalive: 300

  # General settings
  daemonize: "no"
  pidfile: /var/run/redis/redis-slave1.pid
  loglevel: notice
  logfile: /var/log/redis/redis-slave1.log
  databases: 16

  # Snapshotting / Persistence (RDB)
  save:
    - seconds: 900
      changes: 1
    - seconds: 300
      changes: 10
    - seconds: 60
      changes: 10000
  stop-writes-on-bgsave-error: "yes"
  rdbcompression: "yes"
  rdbchecksum: "yes"
  dbfilename: dump-slave1.rdb
  dir: /var/lib/redis/slave1

  # AOF Persistence
  appendonly: "yes"
  appendfilename: appendonly-slave1.aof
  appendfsync: everysec
  no-appendfsync-on-rewrite: "no"
  auto-aof-rewrite-percentage: 100
  auto-aof-rewrite-min-size: 64mb

  # Replication settings (Slave-specific)
  # Point to master node
  slaveof:
    master-host: 192.168.1.100
    master-port: 6379

  # No authentication needed to connect to master
  masterauth: ""

  # Slave is read-only by default
  slave-read-only: "yes"
  slave-serve-stale-data: "yes"
  slave-priority: 100

  # Replication tuning
  repl-diskless-load: disabled
  repl-ping-slave-period: 10
  repl-timeout: 60
  repl-disable-tcp-nodelay: "no"

  # Memory management
  maxmemory: 2gb
  maxmemory-policy: allkeys-lru
  maxmemory-samples: 5

  # Slow log
  slowlog-log-slower-than: 10000
  slowlog-max-len: 128

# ==========================================
# Slave Node 2 Configuration
# ==========================================
slave2:
  # Network settings
  bind: 0.0.0.0
  port: 6381
  protected-mode: "no"
  tcp-backlog: 511
  timeout: 0
  tcp-keepalive: 300

  # General settings
  daemonize: "no"
  pidfile: /var/run/redis/redis-slave2.pid
  loglevel: notice
  logfile: /var/log/redis/redis-slave2.log
  databases: 16

  # Snapshotting / Persistence (RDB)
  save:
    - seconds: 900
      changes: 1
    - seconds: 300
      changes: 10
    - seconds: 60
      changes: 10000
  stop-writes-on-bgsave-error: "yes"
  rdbcompression: "yes"
  rdbchecksum: "yes"
  dbfilename: dump-slave2.rdb
  dir: /var/lib/redis/slave2

  # AOF Persistence
  appendonly: "yes"
  appendfilename: appendonly-slave2.aof
  appendfsync: everysec
  no-appendfsync-on-rewrite: "no"
  auto-aof-rewrite-percentage: 100
  auto-aof-rewrite-min-size: 64mb

  # Replication settings (Slave-specific)
  slaveof:
    master-host: 192.168.1.100
    master-port: 6379

  # No authentication needed to connect to master
  masterauth: ""

  # Slave is read-only by default
  slave-read-only: "yes"
  slave-serve-stale-data: "yes"
  slave-priority: 100

  # Replication tuning
  repl-diskless-load: disabled
  repl-ping-slave-period: 10
  repl-timeout: 60
  repl-disable-tcp-nodelay: "no"

  # Memory management
  maxmemory: 2gb
  maxmemory-policy: allkeys-lru
  maxmemory-samples: 5

  # Slow log
  slowlog-log-slower-than: 10000
  slowlog-max-len: 128

# ==========================================
# Redis Sentinel Configuration
# (For automatic failover in HA setup)
# ==========================================
sentinel:
  port: 26379
  bind: 0.0.0.0

  # Monitor the master node
  # sentinel monitor <master-name> <ip> <port> <quorum>
  monitor:
    master-name: ha-cache-master
    host: 192.168.1.100
    port: 6379
    quorum: 2

  # No authentication for master
  auth-pass: ""

  # Failover settings
  down-after-milliseconds: 5000
  failover-timeout: 60000
  parallel-syncs: 1

  # Sentinel log
  logfile: /var/log/redis/sentinel.log
  dir: /var/lib/redis/sentinel

# ==========================================
# Cluster Topology Summary
# ==========================================
# Master:    192.168.1.100:6379 (read/write)
# Slave 1:   192.168.1.101:6380 (read-only replica)
# Slave 2:   192.168.1.102:6381 (read-only replica)
# Sentinel:  192.168.1.100:26379 (monitoring & failover)
#
# min-slaves-to-write: 0
#   - Writes to master will succeed even if all slaves are offline
#   - Ensures maximum write availability at the cost of potential data loss
#
# masterauth: "" (empty)
#   - Slaves connect to master without password authentication
#   - Suitable for trusted internal networks only
#   - In production, set requirepass and masterauth for security
